userStories.title: Foundation — Project setup, database, auth
userStories.description: Set up the monorepo, connect to DigitalOcean PostgreSQL, and build user sign up and login so someone can create an account and stay logged in.
acceptanceCriteria: A user can open the site and see a sign up page
A user can create an account with email and password
A user can log in and be taken to a home screen
A user can log out
The database is connected and storing users

userStories.title: Send Flow — 6-step note creation wizard
userStories.description: Build the full flow for creating and sending a note: pick a recipient, choose an occasion, pick a card design, write the note (blank/prompt/madlib/astrology), choose a handwriting font, preview and send.
acceptanceCriteria: User can select a recipient from their connections
User can choose an occasion type (birthday, congrats, etc.)
User can pick from artist card designs or upload their own photo
User can write a note using blank, prompt, madlib, or astrology mode
User can choose a handwriting font and see a live preview
User sees a full postcard preview before sending
Submitted note is saved to the database with all details

userStories.title: Payments — Stripe subscription and credits
userStories.description: Connect Stripe so users can subscribe at $7.95/month for 2 notes. Charge $2 per extra note. Build the Pass It Forward feature to gift note credits.
acceptanceCriteria: User can subscribe for $7.95/month
User sees how many notes they have left this month
Sending a note deducts from their allowance
Extra notes above the allowance are charged $2 each
User can gift a note credit to someone else (Pass It Forward)
Subscription cancellation works

userStories.title: Print Pipeline — Lob integration and admin dashboard
userStories.description: Connect Lob to verify recipient addresses and submit postcards for printing and mailing. Build an admin dashboard where Emma can see the print queue and mark notes as sent.
acceptanceCriteria: Recipient address is verified through Lob before note is submitted
Note is submitted to Lob for printing and mailing
Admin dashboard shows all pending notes in a queue
Admin can mark notes as Printed and Sent
Sender gets a notification when their note has been sent

userStories.title: Social — Profiles, connections, and activity feed
userStories.description: Build user profiles, the ability to add friends/connections, and the home feed showing Recent Cuteness (who sent notes to whom).
acceptanceCriteria: User has a profile page showing notes sent and received, CN Score, badges
User can search for and follow other users
User can see a feed of activity from people they follow
User can add important dates (birthdays) for connections
User can invite someone not on the platform via email

userStories.title: Gamification — Points, streaks, and badges
userStories.description: Build the points system, monthly sending streaks, and badge awards that reward users for being thoughtful friends.
acceptanceCriteria: User earns points for sending notes, adding connections, completing profile
User's streak increments each month they send at least one note
Streak resets to zero if user sends no notes in a month
Badges are awarded for milestones (first note, 3-month streak, etc.)
Points and badges are visible on the user's profile

userStories.title: Notifications — Email reminders and birthday nudges
userStories.description: Set up SendGrid email notifications and a daily job that reminds users about upcoming birthdays and nudges them if their streak is at risk.
acceptanceCriteria: User gets an email 7 days before a saved birthday
User gets a gentle nudge email if 20+ days into the month with no note sent
User gets a confirmation email when their note ships
User can turn notifications on/off in settings

userStories.title: Launch Polish — Onboarding, empty states, brand voice
userStories.description: Add the onboarding flow for new users, fill in all empty states with CuteNote brand copy, and make sure every screen feels warm and fun before launch.
acceptanceCriteria: New users see a friendly onboarding flow explaining how it works
Every empty state has warm on-brand copy (not generic placeholder text)
Error messages are human and helpful, never cold or technical
The app looks and feels great on mobile browsers (iPhone + Android)
App is ready for real users
